name: shraga
description: The teammate you delegate coding to — a self-hostable, multi-user AI coding agent web UI (Claude Code, with a pluggable engine seam).
//exports.server: UNSTABLE internal seam (semver-exempt): ./server/* lets a downstream feature (e.g. the Circles Gmail overlay in shraga-circles) import the server-internal modules it already coupled to under the old rsync/overlay model — claude/sessions/contacts/mcp/paths. The stable public surface is '.' (createShraga + feature/engine/webhook/event seams). Depend on these subpaths only if '.' cannot express it, and expect breakage across minors.
author: Elya Livshitz

scripts.dev: concurrently "PORT=3033 bun run --watch src/server/index.ts" "until curl -sf http://localhost:3033/api/version >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 0.2; done; vite --host --port 3032"
scripts.guard:publish:
bun -e "const bad=Object.keys(process.env).filter(k=>/^(VITE_|FIREBASE_CONFIG)/.test(k)&&process.env[k]);if(bad.length){console.error('
✖ Refusing to publish: '+bad.join(', ')+' set in the environment.
  The client build inlines these, so publishing would bake real credentials into dist/.
  Publish from CI (clean env), or re-run the publish under a scrubbed env (env -i).
');process.exit(1)}console.log('✓ publish guard: no client-inlined config in env')"
scripts.prepublishOnly: bun run guard:publish && bun run build
